title: reconr: adaptive over-refinement clusters spurious points at low-E thermal (T42 Zn-67 +1096 pts)
description: Localized by NJOY_jl-xu3 investigation (Phase 91), needs independent confirmation. Julia reconr MF3/MT1 grid for T42 (Zn-67, MAT 3034) = 52307 pts vs Fortran oracle 51211 (+1096). First divergence at grid point ~10, E=1.308719e-5 eV (deep thermal, far below any resonance). Fortran emits ONE point (1.308720-5); Julia explodes to 11 clustered points (1.308719, 1.308721, 1.308729, ... 1.310611; spacings doubling outward = stack-bisection-of-a-converged-interval signature). XS is smooth and values MATCH (Julia 3.305256+2 vs Fortran 3.305254+2, ~6e-7 rel); linear interp at Julia's midpoint 1.310611-5 gives error 1.2e-6 << err=1e-3 -> the bisected points are UNNECESSARY by tolerance. PROVEN independent of 4k2 (reverting _THIRD to 1.0/3.0 leaves the cluster) and e5n (reconr is upstream of broadr; e5n only touched auto_params.jl+modules/broadr.jl). Long-standing. Propagates downstream: inflates broadr/acer/gaspr line counts (the apparent xu3 'regressions'). Site: reconr adaptive reconstruction convergence/min-spacing/bisection-stack logic (src/processing/adaptive_grid.jl, reconr.jl). Likely also affects T28 (Pu-241) and other resolved-resonance tests' line counts -> potentially high sweep-wide DIFFS impact. ACCEPTANCE: Julia reconr T42 MT1 grid == Fortran (no spurious low-E cluster), no regression to 9 BI canaries.
close_reason: Root-caused via instrumented trace (both sides) + fixed surgically. NOT the step guard (identical both sides) nor peak-triplet hw — the seed was lunion_grid over-shading: is_histogram=any(law==Histogram) shaded EVERY interior breakpoint into {sigfig(-1),sigfig(+1)} pairs if the section had ANY histogram region, including log-log breakpoints. Zn-67 MT107 mixed law (NBT/INT 70/5,99/1,190/5 = log-log/histogram/log-log) -> log-log breakpoint 1.308720e-5 wrongly split into {1.308719,1.308721} (Δ2e-11), seeding a tiny panel that resxs/adaptive_reconstruct over-refined (+1096 pts). Fortran reconr.f90:2051 shades only when per-breakpoint inta==1 (histogram). Fix: gate on _inta_for_point(tab,k)==Histogram (new helper mirroring reconr.f90:2019-2023/2076-2080). T42 MT1 52307->51211 (exact Fortran, cluster gone); ALL 9 BI canaries stay BIT_IDENTICAL; 6 resonance canaries unchanged. Surgical (1 file +34/-9). Midpoint confirmed arithmetic both sides (geometric claim refuted). SYSTEMATIC — affects all mixed-law sections, may improve other tests (full sweep pending).

title: build_thermal_grid: under-includes input points + O(n²)-ish cost on dense Bragg (T67 D-LiD 3374 edges)

MF3/MT221+222+223=2357 lines + MF6 NC=35300 follow deterministically from the 7060-pt grid. T70 no regression (213800==ref, matches not decreased), T09 BIT_IDENTICAL preserved. title: broadr: MF3/MT1 thermal-range total — investigate sum-of-broadened-partials vs broaden-the-total (T01 floor / Trap #14)
description:
CONFIRMED vs Fortran + scoped (do NOT ship the narrow version). Fortran broadr label-275 (broadr.f90:938-980): thermal-range (E<=thnmax) MF3/MT1 = sigfig(Σ broadened partials over mtr[], minus iflag channel-dedup, + URR getunx); above thnmax = verbatim original total. Julia currently broadens the TOTAL via sigma1_at (wrong: nonlinear kernel).

PARTIAL FIX (saved /tmp/e5n_partial_sum.patch; broadr.jl:114-145 restructure summing Julia's broadened partials before sigfig) WORKS for light nuclei: T70 MF3/MT1 -> BIT_IDENTICAL (was 471-line diff), T01 32813->32858. BUT REGRESSES B-10 (T45) and Co-58m1 (T82) ~99%: Julia's select_broadr_partials = {MT2,MT18,MT102} is NARROWER than Fortran's mtr[]. EXOTHERMIC non-threshold reactions dominate the thermal total but are not broadened by Julia — B-10 MT107 (n,alpha, ~3840 b @2200m/s = ~99% of thermal MT1), Co-58m1 MT103 (n,p) + MT51 (exothermic de-excitation). Reverted.

COMPLETE FIX (this is the real work): (1) widen select_broadr_partials (auto_params.jl:69-92) to Fortran's full mtr[] membership (broadr.f90:497-560: every reaction with first-E<=emin / exothermic / MT18 / LRF7 partials / sub-eresh for lrp=1); (2) broaden+write all of them (broadn_grid already handles N columns) — note this changes the broadened partial MF3 sections too, matching Fortran; (3) MT1 sum applies iflag channel-dedup (broadr.f90:945-977: exclude MT600-849 when MT103-107 present, etc.) + URR getunx add-back. BROAD regression surface (every test's MF3 partials) -> needs full sweep. At-risk set already mapped: only T45+T82 among the 51 broadr tests have exothermic non-{2,18,102} reactions below thnmax (no MF2/MT152 URR in any resource). Needs a dedicated session.

title: thermr T70: MF1/MT451 directory NC for MF3/MT221+222 = 497 vs ref 498 (section IS 498 — directory metadata bug, not grid)
description: After the Phase-85 terp1 convergence fix, T70 tape60 total=213800 and EVERY section line count matches ref exactly (verified by awk: MF3/MT221=498, MF3/MT222=498, MF6/MT221=185295, MF6/MT222=4). The remaining first structural diff (reference_test.jl first-diff lines 69-70) is in the MF1/MT451 DIRECTORY: Julia writes NC=497 for the MF3/MT221 and MF3/MT222 entries, but the actual sections are 498 lines and the reference directory says 498. So this is a directory-NC computation off-by-one in the PENDF writer, NOT a grid/Bragg-edge bug — the 'grid off-by-one (497 vs 498)' framing inherited from NJOY_jl-7dp/research was a MISREAD of the directory NC value (the MF3 grid is correct at 498). Exactly 2 lines differ. Likely the NC tally for thermal MF3 TAB1 sections omits one record (e.g. the interpolation/INT line: 1 HEAD + 1 TAB1-CONT + 1 INT + 495 data = 498). Read how Fortran tpend/MF1 builds the directory NC for these MTs (LAW 2), fix the NC computation in the pendf_writer MF1/MT451 directory builder. Verify: T70 reference_test lines 69-70 match + total stays 213800; no regression T01/T70.
notes: FIX DECISION (Phase 86 research, 3+1 read-only fan-out, oracle-verified): the bead's own 'use 3+cld(np,3)' suggestion is REJECTED per Rule 1. Fortran thermr tpend (thermr.f90:3087 nc=3+(ne+2)/3; :3198 scr(6)=ne+1; :3211-3213 etop=20e6 sentinel) computes NC with ne=NP_written-1 (one etop sentinel is counted in the written NP but NOT in ne). So the faithful formula on the ACTUAL emitted np is nc = 3 + div(np+1, 3). This DIVERGES from 3+cld(np,3)=3+div(np+2,3) exactly when np≡1 (mod 3) — there Fortran UNDERCOUNTS the true record count by 1, a quirk we must reproduce (GROUND-TRUTH PRINCIPLE). All current tests (T70 np=1485, T68 np=378, T01 np=146) have np NOT≡1 mod3 so both formulas agree, but Fortran-faithful div(np+1,3) is canonical. reconr MF3 (else branch) keeps 3+cld(np,3) — reconr.f90:5115-5116, no sentinel. thermr_coh_ne mechanism is the defect (origin commit 775092c) and is dead after fix → cleanup follow-up. Empirical ref: T70 MT221+222 NC=498 (referenceTape60 lines 69-70), T68 MT221 NC=129, T01 MT221 NC=52.
close_reason: Fixed: thermr MF3 directory NC now uses faithful Fortran tpend formula 3+div(np+1,3) on the actual emitted np (helper _thermr_mf3_dir_nc), correcting T70 MF3/MT221+222 from NC=497 to 498 (matches referenceTape60 lines 69-70).
